task_completion.description: Navigate to the final grades entry screen for the correct course and marking period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenSIS interface showing the grades entry screen. The selected course must be 'World History', Period '1', and the marking period must be 'FY' or 'Full Year'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct grade for James Miller.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grading table, the row for 'James Miller' shows '92' in the percentage/score input field and 'A' in the letter grade dropdown/field.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct grade for Sarah Wilson.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grading table, the row for 'Sarah Wilson' shows '85' in the percentage/score input field and 'B' in the letter grade dropdown/field.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct grade for Robert Chen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grading table, the row for 'Robert Chen' shows '76' in the percentage/score input field and 'C' in the letter grade dropdown/field.

task_completion.description: Enter the required comment for Robert Chen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the grading table, the row for 'Robert Chen' contains the text 'Needs improvement in homework submission.' in the comments input field.

task_completion.description: Save or post the entered grades.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating that the grades have been saved/posted, or visual confirmation that the 'Save'/'Submit' button was clicked and the entered data persists on the screen.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenSIS web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a standard web browser navigating the OpenSIS application, not a text editor or a static mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or direct database queries to insert the grades.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing 'mysql' insert commands, 'curl' requests, or script executions to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Elements/Console tab) should not be open and actively used to modify the DOM text to simulate entered grades.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Course Name: World History. Student 1: James Miller (Expected: 92%, Grade A). Student 2: Sarah Wilson (Expected: 85%, Grade B). Student 3: Robert Chen (Expected: 76%, Grade C, Comment must include 'Needs improvement').
